title: Benchmarking Small Language Models and Small Reasoning Language Models on System Log Severity Classification
abstract: This benchmark evaluates small, deployable language models for system log severity classification, addressing real-time requirements of digital twin systems. The study demonstrates that severity classification serves as a lens for evaluating model competence and real-time deployability, with implications for root cause analysis and broader digital twin integration.

methodology.focus: Small, deployable language models for real-time requirements

severity_values.purpose: Quantifies risk level of each log

challenges_addressed: Manual interpretation of massive log volumes
